instructions.instruction: Use this skill when the task is to write, review, or clean up RSpec tests.
instructions.original_snippets: Use this skill when the task is to write, review, or clean up RSpec tests.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger write-tests.

instructions.instruction: **Core principle:** Prefer behavioral confidence over implementation coupling. Good specs are readable, deterministic, and cheap to maintain.
instructions.original_snippets: **Core principle:** Prefer behavioral confidence over implementation coupling. Good specs are readable, deterministic, and cheap to maintain.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger write-tests.

instructions.instruction: **Write the failing spec** — pick the smallest spec type that exercises the intended behaviour (model > service > request > system).
instructions.original_snippets: **Write the failing spec** — pick the smallest spec type that exercises the intended behaviour (model > service > request > system).
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger write-tests.

instructions.instruction: **Run it and confirm the failure message** — the error should be about missing code, not a setup problem.
instructions.original_snippets: **Run it and confirm the failure message** — the error should be about missing code, not a setup problem.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger write-tests.

instructions.instruction: **Verify** — run the full relevant spec file, then the suite, before committing.
instructions.original_snippets: **Verify** — run the full relevant spec file, then the suite, before committing.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger write-tests.

instructions.instruction: Minimal factories only. Never rely on factory defaults for business logic — set explicitly or use traits. Avoid `create` when `build`/`build_stubbed` suffices.
instructions.original_snippets: Minimal factories only. Never rely on factory defaults for business logic — set explicitly or use traits. Avoid `create` when `build`/`build_stubbed` suffices.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger write-tests.

instructions.instruction: Use only when the same behavioural contract applies to multiple subjects without per-example `let` overrides. Avoid when each context needs different setup — that signals a wrong abstraction.
instructions.original_snippets: Use only when the same behavioural contract applies to multiple subjects without per-example `let` overrides. Avoid when each context needs different setup — that signals a wrong abstraction.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger write-tests.

instructions.instruction: When asked to write or review RSpec specs, your output MUST satisfy each rule below. Each is graded independently — one violation drops the whole check.
instructions.original_snippets: When asked to write or review RSpec specs, your output MUST satisfy each rule below. Each is graded independently — one violation drops the whole check.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger write-tests.

instructions.instruction: **`let` for test data**, `let!` ONLY when the object must exist before the action under test.
instructions.original_snippets: **`let` for test data**, `let!` ONLY when the object must exist before the action under test.
instructions.relevant_when: When a user asks for Rails work that should trigger write-tests.
